question:
Given the following 12 rounds of dice results, find an optimal category assignment.

Scoring rules (use these exact point values):
- Full House: 25 points
- Small Straight: 30 points
- Large Straight: 40 points
- Yacht: 50 points
- Aces through Sixes: sum of dice showing that number
- Three-of-a-Kind / Four-of-a-Kind: sum of all dice if the dice qualify
- Upper section bonus: +35 when the sum of Aces through Sixes is 63 or more

Round 1: [4, 1, 2, 1, 2]
Round 2: [3, 5, 1, 3, 5]
Round 3: [4, 1, 3, 3, 1]
Round 4: [5, 1, 1, 1, 5]
Round 5: [5, 6, 5, 6, 4]
Round 6: [1, 2, 2, 6, 6]
Round 7: [4, 4, 1, 4, 4]
Round 8: [1, 1, 4, 6, 4]
Round 9: [1, 4, 4, 6, 6]
Round 10: [1, 5, 1, 5, 3]
Round 11: [5, 6, 6, 2, 2]
Round 12: [6, 3, 4, 4, 6]

Compute the optimal assignment. If additional instructions below specify particular rounds, report only the requested partial sum—not the full 12-round total.

IMPORTANT — Spotcheck grading (not the full 12-round total):
First compute an optimal assignment that maximizes the usual total score over all 12 rounds (same rules and bonus as always). Using that optimal assignment, output ONLY the sum of the per-round scores for rounds 4, 7. Your final line must be exactly `Answer: <integer>` with that partial sum.

Scoring rules (use these exact point values):
- Full House: 25 points
- Small Straight: 30 points
- Large Straight: 40 points
- Yacht: 50 points
- Aces through Sixes: sum of dice showing that number
- Three-of-a-Kind / Four-of-a-Kind: sum of all dice if the dice qualify
- Upper section bonus: +35 when the sum of Aces through Sixes is 63 or more

Round 1: [6, 3, 5, 6, 5]
Round 2: [3, 5, 5, 1, 3]
Round 3: [3, 3, 1, 1, 4]
Round 4: [1, 1, 6, 3, 6]
Round 5: [5, 5, 2, 3, 2]
Round 6: [1, 1, 4, 1, 4]
Round 7: [5, 3, 3, 5, 1]
Round 8: [1, 6, 3, 6, 3]
Round 9: [4, 4, 1, 1, 6]
Round 10: [3, 6, 5, 6, 5]
Round 11: [4, 6, 4, 4, 4]
Round 12: [2, 2, 4, 4, 3]

Compute the optimal assignment. If additional instructions below specify particular rounds, report only the requested partial sum—not the full 12-round total.

First compute an optimal assignment that maximizes the usual total score over all 12 rounds (same rules and bonus as always). Using that optimal assignment, output ONLY the sum of the per-round scores for rounds 6, 11. Your final line must be exactly `Answer: <integer>` with that partial sum.

- Large Straight: 40 points

- Three-of-a-Kind / Four-of-a-Kind: sum of all dice if the dice qualify
- Upper section bonus: +35 when the sum of Aces through Sixes is 63 or more

Given the following 12 rounds of dice results, find an optimal category assignment.

Scoring rules (use these exact point values):
- Full House: 25 points
- Small Straight: 30 points
- Large Straight: 40 points
- Yacht: 50 points
- Aces through Sixes: sum of dice showing that number
- Three-of-a-Kind / Four-of-a-Kind: sum of all dice if the dice qualify
- Upper section bonus: +35 when the sum of Aces through Sixes is 63 or more

Round 1: [5, 3, 2, 5, 2]
Round 2: [4, 6, 2, 4, 2]
Round 3: [2, 2, 1, 6, 1]
Round 4: [2, 3, 3, 4, 3]
Round 5: [1, 5, 4, 5, 1]
Round 6: [6, 6, 2, 3, 3]
Round 7: [6, 1, 1, 3, 6]
Round 8: [2, 5, 6, 5, 2]
Round 9: [6, 4, 6, 5, 5]
Round 10: [5, 2, 4, 5, 4]
Round 11: [1, 6, 6, 1, 1]
Round 12: [6, 1, 3, 3, 1]

Compute the optimal assignment. If additional instructions below specify particular rounds, report only the requested partial sum—not the full 12-round total.

IMPORTANT — Spotcheck grading (not the full 12-round total):
First compute an optimal assignment that maximizes the usual total score over all 12 rounds (same rules and bonus as always). Using that optimal assignment, output ONLY the sum of the per-round scores for rounds 4, 11. Your final line must be exactly `Answer: <integer>` with that partial sum.

Scoring rules (use these exact point values):
- Full House: 25 points
- Small Straight: 30 points
- Large Straight: 40 points
- Yacht: 50 points
- Aces through Sixes: sum of dice showing that number
- Three-of-a-Kind / Four-of-a-Kind: sum of all dice if the dice qualify
- Upper section bonus: +35 when the sum of Aces through Sixes is 63 or more

Round 1: [1, 1, 6, 3, 3]
Round 2: [1, 3, 1, 2, 2]
Round 3: [1, 5, 2, 5, 2]

Round 5: [6, 1, 3, 3, 1]
Round 6: [5, 5, 6, 4, 5]
Round 7: [5, 5, 4, 4, 3]
Round 8: [5, 2, 2, 5, 6]
Round 9: [6, 6, 2, 2, 5]
Round 10: [4, 5, 4, 2, 2]
Round 11: [3, 1, 1, 1, 3]
Round 12: [3, 4, 3, 4, 1]

Compute the optimal assignment. If additional instructions below specify particular rounds, report only the requested partial sum—not the full 12-round total.

IMPORTANT — Spotcheck grading (not the full 12-round total):
First compute an optimal assignment that maximizes the usual total score over all 12 rounds (same rules and bonus as always). Using that optimal assignment, output ONLY the sum of the per-round scores for rounds 6, 11. Your final line must be exactly `Answer: <integer>` with that partial sum.

Given the following 12 rounds of dice results, find an optimal category assignment.

Scoring rules (use these exact point values):
- Full House: 25 points
- Small Straight: 30 points
- Large Straight: 40 points
- Yacht: 50 points
- Aces through Sixes: sum of dice showing that number
- Three-of-a-Kind / Four-of-a-Kind: sum of all dice if the dice qualify
- Upper section bonus: +35 when the sum of Aces through Sixes is 63 or more

Round 1: [5, 6, 4, 4, 5]
Round 2: [4, 4, 2, 2, 6]
Round 3: [3, 6, 3, 5, 6]
Round 4: [1, 3, 2, 2, 3]
Round 5: [2, 1, 1, 1, 2]
Round 6: [3, 2, 3, 5, 5]
Round 7: [2, 4, 2, 3, 3]
Round 8: [3, 4, 4, 6, 3]
Round 9: [2, 1, 2, 5, 5]
Round 10: [1, 4, 1, 3, 3]
Round 11: [1, 5, 1, 5, 6]
Round 12: [4, 6, 4, 4, 4]

Compute the optimal assignment. If additional instructions below specify particular rounds, report only the requested partial sum—not the full 12-round total.

IMPORTANT — Spotcheck grading (not the full 12-round total):
First compute an optimal assignment that maximizes the usual total score over all 12 rounds (same rules and bonus as always). Using that optimal assignment, output ONLY the sum of the per-round scores for rounds 5, 12. Your final line must be exactly `Answer: <integer>` with that partial sum.

Round 10: [3, 2, 3, 3, 3]
Round 11: [2, 5, 2, 5, 4]
Round 12: [2, 1, 1, 4, 4]

Compute the optimal assignment. If additional instructions below specify particular rounds, report only the requested partial sum—not the full 12-round total.

First compute an optimal assignment that maximizes the usual total score over all 12 rounds (same rules and bonus as always). Using that optimal assignment, output ONLY the sum of the per-round scores for rounds 8, 10. Your final line must be exactly `Answer: <integer>` with that partial sum.

question:
Given the following 12 rounds of dice results, find an optimal category assignment.

Scoring rules (use these exact point values):
- Full House: 25 points
- Small Straight: 30 points
- Large Straight: 40 points
- Yacht: 50 points
- Aces through Sixes: sum of dice showing that number
- Three-of-a-Kind / Four-of-a-Kind: sum of all dice if the dice qualify
- Upper section bonus: +35 when the sum of Aces through Sixes is 63 or more

Round 1: [3, 6, 2, 5, 4]
Round 2: [3, 2, 1, 5, 4]
Round 3: [5, 1, 3, 2, 4]
Round 4: [3, 4, 6, 5, 3]
Round 5: [4, 4, 4, 4, 4]
Round 6: [1, 2, 4, 6, 3]
Round 7: [3, 6, 4, 5, 2]
Round 8: [6, 3, 4, 2, 5]
Round 9: [3, 3, 3, 3, 3]
Round 10: [5, 2, 3, 4, 1]
Round 11: [5, 5, 5, 5, 5]
Round 12: [2, 1, 5, 4, 3]

Compute the optimal assignment. If additional instructions below specify particular rounds, report only the requested partial sum—not the full 12-round total.

IMPORTANT — Spotcheck grading (not the full 12-round total):
First compute an optimal assignment that maximizes the usual total score over all 12 rounds (same rules and bonus as always). Using that optimal assignment, output ONLY the sum of the per-round scores for rounds 3, 5. Your final line must be exactly `Answer: <integer>` with that partial sum.

- Large Straight: 40 points
- Yacht: 50 points
- Aces through Sixes: sum of dice showing that number
- Three-of-a-Kind / Four-of-a-Kind: sum of all dice if the dice qualify
- Upper section bonus: +35 when the sum of Aces through Sixes is 63 or more

Round 9: [2, 1, 4, 3, 1]
Round 10: [6, 3, 4, 4, 5]
Round 11: [6, 4, 4, 6, 4]
Round 12: [4, 4, 2, 3, 1]

Given the following 12 rounds of dice results, find an optimal category assignment.

Scoring rules (use these exact point values):
- Full House: 25 points
- Small Straight: 30 points
- Large Straight: 40 points
- Yacht: 50 points
- Aces through Sixes: sum of dice showing that number
- Three-of-a-Kind / Four-of-a-Kind: sum of all dice if the dice qualify
- Upper section bonus: +35 when the sum of Aces through Sixes is 63 or more

Round 1: [2, 6, 5, 3, 4]
Round 2: [3, 2, 2, 2, 1]
Round 3: [2, 2, 2, 2, 2]
Round 4: [3, 5, 4, 5, 2]
Round 5: [5, 5, 3, 5, 3]
Round 6: [3, 4, 5, 4, 6]
Round 7: [5, 1, 5, 5, 1]
Round 8: [5, 6, 2, 3, 4]
Round 9: [5, 2, 6, 3, 4]
Round 10: [3, 3, 3, 3, 3]
Round 11: [3, 2, 2, 4, 5]
Round 12: [3, 5, 6, 2, 4]

Compute the optimal assignment. If additional instructions below specify particular rounds, report only the requested partial sum—not the full 12-round total.

IMPORTANT — Spotcheck grading (not the full 12-round total):
First compute an optimal assignment that maximizes the usual total score over all 12 rounds (same rules and bonus as always). Using that optimal assignment, output ONLY the sum of the per-round scores for rounds 1, 4. Your final line must be exactly `Answer: <integer>` with that partial sum.

- Full House: 25 points
- Small Straight: 30 points
- Large Straight: 40 points
- Yacht: 50 points
- Aces through Sixes: sum of dice showing that number
- Three-of-a-Kind / Four-of-a-Kind: sum of all dice if the dice qualify
- Upper section bonus: +35 when the sum of Aces through Sixes is 63 or more

Round 1: [5, 4, 1, 4, 1]
Round 2: [1, 1, 3, 3, 2]
Round 3: [6, 6, 4, 3, 6]
Round 4: [6, 2, 2, 5, 5]
Round 5: [6, 3, 2, 2, 3]
Round 6: [1, 4, 4, 6, 6]
Round 7: [1, 6, 1, 6, 1]
Round 8: [5, 3, 5, 3, 4]
Round 9: [4, 5, 4, 1, 5]
Round 10: [6, 5, 3, 6, 5]
Round 11: [2, 4, 6, 4, 6]
Round 12: [6, 5, 1, 6, 5]

Compute the optimal assignment. If additional instructions below specify particular rounds, report only the requested partial sum—not the full 12-round total.

First compute an optimal assignment that maximizes the usual total score over all 12 rounds (same rules and bonus as always). Using that optimal assignment, output ONLY the sum of the per-round scores for rounds 3, 7. Your final line must be exactly `Answer: <integer>` with that partial sum.

Scoring rules (use these exact point values):
- Full House: 25 points

- Large Straight: 40 points
- Yacht: 50 points
- Aces through Sixes: sum of dice showing that number
- Three-of-a-Kind / Four-of-a-Kind: sum of all dice if the dice qualify
- Upper section bonus: +35 when the sum of Aces through Sixes is 63 or more

Round 10: [1, 6, 1, 6, 1]
Round 11: [5, 4, 3, 2, 6]
Round 12: [4, 6, 2, 3, 5]

Compute the optimal assignment. If additional instructions below specify particular rounds, report only the requested partial sum—not the full 12-round total.

First compute an optimal assignment that maximizes the usual total score over all 12 rounds (same rules and bonus as always). Using that optimal assignment, output ONLY the sum of the per-round scores for rounds 4, 10. Your final line must be exactly `Answer: <integer>` with that partial sum.

- Upper section bonus: +35 when the sum of Aces through Sixes is 63 or more

question:
Given the following 12 rounds of dice results, find an optimal category assignment.

Scoring rules (use these exact point values):
- Full House: 25 points
- Small Straight: 30 points
- Large Straight: 40 points
- Yacht: 50 points
- Aces through Sixes: sum of dice showing that number
- Three-of-a-Kind / Four-of-a-Kind: sum of all dice if the dice qualify
- Upper section bonus: +35 when the sum of Aces through Sixes is 63 or more

Round 1: [6, 5, 1, 1, 6]
Round 2: [6, 4, 4, 3, 6]
Round 3: [2, 5, 2, 6, 5]
Round 4: [1, 3, 5, 3, 5]
Round 5: [5, 5, 3, 3, 6]
Round 6: [4, 6, 2, 4, 2]
Round 7: [3, 2, 2, 3, 1]
Round 8: [3, 1, 3, 1, 1]
Round 9: [5, 4, 5, 5, 2]
Round 10: [4, 5, 2, 5, 2]
Round 11: [1, 3, 3, 1, 4]
Round 12: [2, 2, 6, 6, 5]

Compute the optimal assignment. If additional instructions below specify particular rounds, report only the requested partial sum—not the full 12-round total.

IMPORTANT — Spotcheck grading (not the full 12-round total):
First compute an optimal assignment that maximizes the usual total score over all 12 rounds (same rules and bonus as always). Using that optimal assignment, output ONLY the sum of the per-round scores for rounds 8, 9. Your final line must be exactly `Answer: <integer>` with that partial sum.

question:
Given the following 12 rounds of dice results, find an optimal category assignment.

Scoring rules (use these exact point values):
- Full House: 25 points
- Small Straight: 30 points
- Large Straight: 40 points
- Yacht: 50 points
- Aces through Sixes: sum of dice showing that number
- Three-of-a-Kind / Four-of-a-Kind: sum of all dice if the dice qualify
- Upper section bonus: +35 when the sum of Aces through Sixes is 63 or more

Round 1: [5, 4, 1, 4, 5]
Round 2: [2, 1, 5, 1, 5]
Round 3: [4, 2, 3, 3, 4]
Round 4: [2, 5, 5, 3, 2]
Round 5: [3, 6, 5, 3, 6]
Round 6: [1, 2, 2, 1, 5]
Round 7: [1, 6, 6, 1, 1]
Round 8: [5, 4, 5, 4, 1]
Round 9: [5, 5, 3, 2, 3]
Round 10: [6, 6, 5, 2, 6]
Round 11: [5, 1, 1, 2, 5]
Round 12: [6, 3, 6, 1, 3]

Compute the optimal assignment. If additional instructions below specify particular rounds, report only the requested partial sum—not the full 12-round total.

IMPORTANT — Spotcheck grading (not the full 12-round total):
First compute an optimal assignment that maximizes the usual total score over all 12 rounds (same rules and bonus as always). Using that optimal assignment, output ONLY the sum of the per-round scores for rounds 7, 10. Your final line must be exactly `Answer: <integer>` with that partial sum.

- Upper section bonus: +35 when the sum of Aces through Sixes is 63 or more

question:
Given the following 12 rounds of dice results, find an optimal category assignment.

Scoring rules (use these exact point values):
- Full House: 25 points
- Small Straight: 30 points
- Large Straight: 40 points
- Yacht: 50 points
- Aces through Sixes: sum of dice showing that number
- Three-of-a-Kind / Four-of-a-Kind: sum of all dice if the dice qualify
- Upper section bonus: +35 when the sum of Aces through Sixes is 63 or more

Round 1: [5, 3, 5, 6, 6]
Round 2: [6, 6, 5, 3, 3]
Round 3: [1, 3, 3, 1, 1]
Round 4: [5, 6, 3, 5, 3]
Round 5: [3, 3, 1, 1, 5]
Round 6: [4, 5, 2, 5, 5]
Round 7: [1, 3, 3, 1, 5]
Round 8: [2, 4, 3, 2, 4]
Round 9: [6, 2, 6, 4, 4]
Round 10: [5, 1, 5, 2, 2]
Round 11: [4, 2, 4, 1, 1]
Round 12: [2, 2, 1, 1, 6]

Compute the optimal assignment. If additional instructions below specify particular rounds, report only the requested partial sum—not the full 12-round total.

IMPORTANT — Spotcheck grading (not the full 12-round total):
First compute an optimal assignment that maximizes the usual total score over all 12 rounds (same rules and bonus as always). Using that optimal assignment, output ONLY the sum of the per-round scores for rounds 3, 6. Your final line must be exactly `Answer: <integer>` with that partial sum.

We need to solve Yahtzee (Yacht Dice) assignment problem with 12 rounds, 13 categories? Actually typical Yahtzee has 13 categories: Aces (Ones) through Sixes (6 categories), Three of a kind, Four of a kind, Full House, Small straight, Large straight, Yacht (Yahtzee), Chance (sum of dice). But in this problem, they didn't list Chance; also missing category "Chance". They gave scoring rules: Full House 25, Small Straight 30, Large Straight 40, Yacht 50, Aces through Sixes sum, Three-of-a-Kind / Four-of-a-Kind: sum of all dice if the dice qualify, Upper section bonus +35 when sum of Aces through Sixes is 63 or more. No mention of Chance, maybe not present. Also they have only 12 rounds and categories probably exactly 12 (the ones listed). List categories: Aces, Twos, Threes, Fours, Fives, Sixes (6); Three of a kind, Four of a kind (2); Full House (1); Small Straight (1); Large Straight (1); Yacht (1). That's total 12 categories. Good matches 12 rounds. So each category used exactly once.

Goal: maximize total score. Then compute sum of scores for round 3 and round 6 under optimal assignment.

We need to compute each round's possible score for each category (if qualifies; otherwise score 0? Usually you must assign a category even if you don't meet, you score 0. So we can assign any round to any category, but if not qualifying, you get 0 for that category, except for upper categories you get sum of dice for that number (could be 0). For three/four of a kind, you get sum all dice only if you have at least three / four of a kind; otherwise score 0.

Let's enumerate rounds.

I'll label dice.

R1: [5,3,5,6,6]
Counts: 5x2, 3x1,6x2. No three of a kind, no four. Not full house (full house is 3+2). It is two pairs. Not straight (small straight needs sequence of at least four distinct values). Values: 3,5,6. Not consecutive 4 distinct. So small straight? The dice: have 3,5,6, plus duplicates. Need 4 consecutive numbers like 1-2-3-4,2-3-4-5,3-4-5-6. Do we have 3,4,5,6? missing 4, so no. Large straight no. Yacht no. Upper categories: sum of 1's =0, 2's =0,3's=3,4's=0,5's=10,6's=12.

R2: [6,6,5,3,3]
Counts: 6x2,3x2,5x1. No three/four. No full house (needs 3+2). No straight (values 3,5,6). Not small straight (need sequence 3,4,5,6 missing 4). Upper sums: Aces0, Twos0, Threes =3+3=6, Fours0, Fives=5, Sixes=12.

R3: [1,3,3,1,1]
Counts: 1x3,3x2. That's a full house (3 of a kind + a pair). Also qualifies for three of a kind and maybe four? not four. So possible scores: Full House 25, Three of a kind sum=1+3+3+1+1=9, Four of a kind not (0), Upper: Aces sum =1+1+1 =3, Twos=0, Threes=3+3=6, others 0.

R4: [5,6,3,5,3]
Counts: 5x2,6x1,3x2. Two pairs again. Upper: 3's=6,5's=10,6's=6.

R5: [3,3,1,1,5]
Counts: 3x2,1x2,5x1. Two pairs. Upper: Ones=2, Threes=6, Fives=5.

- Full House: 25 points
- Small Straight: 30 points
- Large Straight: 40 points
- Yacht: 50 points
- Aces through Sixes: sum of dice showing that number
- Three-of-a-Kind / Four-of-a-Kind: sum of all dice if the dice qualify
- Upper section bonus: +35 when the sum of Aces through Sixes is 63 or more

Round 5: [4, 4, 6, 1, 1]
Round 6: [4, 1, 1, 3, 3]
Round 7: [6, 4, 5, 5, 4]
Round 8: [5, 4, 4, 6, 6]
Round 9: [6, 6, 1, 1, 2]
Round 10: [2, 5, 5, 4, 5]
Round 11: [2, 5, 6, 6, 5]
Round 12: [1, 1, 5, 6, 6]

Compute the optimal assignment. If additional instructions below specify particular rounds, report only the requested partial sum—not the full 12-round total.

First compute an optimal assignment that maximizes the usual total score over all 12 rounds (same rules and bonus as always). Using that optimal assignment, output ONLY the sum of the per-round scores for rounds 2, 10. Your final line must be exactly `Answer: <integer>` with that partial sum.

Given the following 12 rounds of dice results, find an optimal category assignment.

Scoring rules (use these exact point values):
- Full House: 25 points
- Small Straight: 30 points
- Large Straight: 40 points
- Yacht: 50 points
- Aces through Sixes: sum of dice showing that number
- Three-of-a-Kind / Four-of-a-Kind: sum of all dice if the dice qualify
- Upper section bonus: +35 when the sum of Aces through Sixes is 63 or more

Round 1: [4, 6, 4, 4, 1]
Round 2: [5, 5, 2, 2, 4]
Round 3: [4, 3, 3, 4, 2]

Round 5: [2, 2, 3, 6, 6]
Round 6: [3, 6, 6, 1, 1]
Round 7: [1, 2, 2, 1, 3]
Round 8: [6, 5, 4, 4, 6]
Round 9: [4, 3, 3, 6, 6]
Round 10: [5, 3, 5, 2, 2]
Round 11: [6, 6, 6, 6, 2]
Round 12: [4, 2, 4, 1, 2]

Compute the optimal assignment. If additional instructions below specify particular rounds, report only the requested partial sum—not the full 12-round total.

First compute an optimal assignment that maximizes the usual total score over all 12 rounds (same rules and bonus as always). Using that optimal assignment, output ONLY the sum of the per-round scores for rounds 1, 11. Your final line must be exactly `Answer: <integer>` with that partial sum.
